title: philotheca myoporoides subsp. leichhardtii
intro: Philotheca myoporoides subsp. leichhardtii, also known as Leichhardt's waxflower or Leichhardt's wax-flower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It is a small to medium-sized shrub native to the eastern coast of Australia. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties. Leichhardt's waxflower has been used to prepare herbal remedies such as teas, tinctures, and ointments, which are said to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and promoting wound healing.
